title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs message envelopes, binary bodies, schemas, and evolution from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.
Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 7912 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

Inspect the Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review messaging team handles case 3387 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A valid AsyncAPI document proves deployed routing and message behavior conform.
